---
title: "Chapter title"
description: "One sentence stating the chapter's engineering purpose."
chapter-id: Z00
status: outline
---

## The system question

Open with an observable behavior, discrepancy, failure, or real design need.
Collect a prediction before introducing the model.

## What you will be able to do

Write three to five observable outcomes. Name the prerequisite idea that each
outcome builds on. Avoid generic outcomes that could be pasted into every
chapter.

## Build the model

Define quantities, reference directions, units, assumptions, and the model's
domain of validity. Connect the physical view, circuit or architecture, words,
and equations.

## Work an example

Estimate, calculate, check units and limiting cases, then interpret the result.

## Test the model

Compare prediction, simulation where useful, and measured or authentic data.
Record configuration, raw evidence, and uncertainty.

## When the model fails

Develop non-ideal effects, common misconceptions, and one plausible fault through
discriminating evidence.

## Make a design decision

Give measurable requirements, alternatives, trade-offs, an acceptance test, and
the limit of the resulting claim.

## Summary

Connect the chapter's mental model to its downstream chapters.

## Exercises

Include retrieval, estimation, derivation, data interpretation, debugging, and
open design. Keep live-assessment variants and solutions outside the public book.

## References

Cite claims at the point of use and prefer primary technical sources for
version-bound or consequential facts.
